entries.specific_competence: (b) Explain Avogadro’s law in relation to molar quantities
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Guide student in manageable groups to use the Avogadros Law in explaining the molar quantities
entries.assessment: The Avogadro’s Law is clearly explained and correctly applied in calculating molar quantities
resources: Scientific calculator and analytical balance
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (d) Describe the applications of volumetric analysis in real life and industrial settings as in soft drinks and cocktail making, medicine and in agriculture
entries.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students to perform experiments related to the applications of volumetric analysis
entries.assessment: The applications of volumetric analysis in real life and industrial settings are clearly described
resources: Pipettes, burettes, volumetric flasks, beakers, distilled water, petri dish, watch glass, funnels, wash bottles, backing powder, vinegar, processed juice, antacids tablets and suspensions, fertiliser, NaOH, HCl, H2SO4 , HNO3, CH3COOH, Ca(OH)2, Na2CO3 and NaHCO3
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (e) Explore the basic tenets of ionic theory and electrolysis (ionic theory, mechanism of electrolysis and laws of electrolysis)
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to describe the concept of ionic theory and electrolysis and make presentations Experimentation: Guide students to carry-out experiments that demonstrate the mechanism of electrolysis using simple available chemicals and apparatus Field Trip: Guide students to carry-out fields trips to near by industries involved in extraction, electroplating and purification of metals and examine the applications of electrolysis
entries.assessment: The basic principles of ionic theory and electrolysis are clearly described
resources: Electrolytic cells, pencils, battery (dry cells), connecting wire, distilled water, acid, H2SO4, CuSO4, ZnSO4, NaCl, KCl, and simulations or videos, diagrams, pictures, wall charts, illustrating principles of ionic theory and electrolysis
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (g) Describe the oxidizing and displacement properties of non-metal compounds (oxygen, hydrogen, chlorine, hydrogen chloride, sulphur dioxide, nitrogen, ammonia, and carbon dioxide)
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to describe the properties of non-metals and write report Project works: Guide students to carry-out visits to nearby industries for making gases, pesticides, fertilisers and other chemicals, analyse properties of the compounds of non-metals
entries.assessment: Oxidizing and displacement properties of non-metal compounds are clearly described
resources: Wall charts and pictures showing some important oxidation reactions, periodic table, oxygen gas, hydrogen gas, chlorine gas, hydrogen chloride gas, sulphur dioxide gas, nitrogen gas, ammonia gas, carbon dioxide gas, coloured flower, dyes, hydrogen sulphide, Iron(II) chloride, potassium dichromate and potassium permanganate
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Conduct experiments in Chemistry (a) Perform experiments on volumetric analysis
entries.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students to perform titrations for various standard solutions Field trips: Guide students to execute a field trips to nearby industries mainly concerned with quantification of species in different substances and write a report for assessment
entries.assessment: Experiments on volumetric analysis are performed correctly
resources: Analytical balance, pipette, burette, volumetric flasks, beakers, distilled water, laboratory glassware, funnels, wash bottles, NaOH, HCl, H2SO4, HNO3, CH3COOH, Ca(OH)2, Na2CO3 and NaHCO3
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.assessment: Experiments on volumetric analysis are performed correctly

entries.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students to perform titrations for various standard solutions Field trips: Guide students to execute a field trips to nearby industries mainly concerned with quantification of species in different substances and write a report for assessment

entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (b) Perform experiments on the applications of electrolysis in corrosion inhibition and purification of metals
entries.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students to investigate the applications of electrolysis in the electroplating process for common items in the environment
entries.assessment: Experiments on the applications of electrolysis in corrosion, inhibition and purification of metals are correctly performed
resources: Graph papers, sand papers, dry cells Li-ion (3 V &6 V), Lead acid cells (12 V , 5 Ah-7 Ah), Sulphuric acid, copper(II) sulphate, copper ores and sheets, spoon, copper wires, sodium chloride, graphite rods plate, aluminium ores, gold, zinc, impure copper plates, chloride/ sulphate, iron plates, distilled water, potassium dichromate, coulometer, pure copper rods, ammeter, voltmeter, and 100 mL and 250 mL beakers
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (c) Use appropriate software to sketch the set-ups of experiments
entries.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students to use various software in sketching experimental set-ups
entries.assessment: The set-ups of experiments are correctly sketched using Chemistry software
resources: Chemistry-based computer software
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (e) Demonstrate oxidizing and displacement properties of non-metallic compounds (oxygen, hydrogen, chlorine, hydrogen chloride, sulphur dioxide, nitrogen, ammonia and carbon dioxide)
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to describe properties of non-metal compounds and make presentations Inquiry-based learning: Guide students to investigate industrial mechanisms involving oxidizing and displacement properties of non-metals
entries.assessment: Oxidizing and displacement properties of non-metal compounds are correctly demonstrated
resources: Periodic table, oxygen gas, hydrogen gas, chlorine gas, hydrogen chloride gas, sulphur dioxide gas, nitrogen gas, ammonia gas, carbon dioxide gas, coloured flower, dyes, hydrogen sulphide, iron (II) chloride, potassium dichromate and potassium permanganate and videos, animations or simulations, wall charts and pictures illustrating some important oxidation reactions
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (f) Prepare sulphuric acid from sulphur dioxide
entries.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students to prepare sulphuric acid by using sulphur dioxide
entries.assessment: Sulphuric acid from sulphur dioxide is correctly prepared
resources: Wall charts showing the laboratory / industrial preparation of sulphuric acid, sulphur dioxide, distilled water, delivery tubes and flasks with side arms
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic principles of extraction of metals
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Demonstrate mastery of the principles of extraction of metals (a) Describe the extraction of iron from its ore (ores, stages, and methods of extraction)
entries.learning_activity: Field trips: Organise students field trips to nearby mining sites/ factories to analyse the process for extraction of iron from its ores and write the report Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to describe stages and methods involved in extraction of metals and make presentation
entries.assessment: The methods for iron extraction from its ore are clearly described
resources: Wall charts showing the metals ores and their methods for extraction, and real ores for metals present in Tanzania
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic principles of extraction of metals
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Demonstrate mastery of the principles of extraction of metals (a) Describe the extraction of iron from its ore (ores, stages, and methods of extraction)
entries.learning_activity: Field trips: Organise students field trips to nearby mining sites/ factories to analyse the process for extraction of iron from its ores and write the report Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to describe stages and methods involved in extraction of metals and make presentation
entries.assessment: The methods for iron extraction from its ore are clearly described
resources: Wall charts showing the metals ores and their methods for extraction, and real ores for metals present in Tanzania
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic principles of extraction of metals
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Demonstrate mastery of the principles of extraction of metals (a) Describe the extraction of iron from its ore (ores, stages, and methods of extraction)
entries.learning_activity: Field trips: Organise students field trips to nearby mining sites/ factories to analyse the process for extraction of iron from its ores and write the report Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to describe stages and methods involved in extraction of metals and make presentation
entries.assessment: The methods for iron extraction from its ore are clearly described
resources: Wall charts showing the metals ores and their methods for extraction, and real ores for metals present in Tanzania
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Conduct a project in Chemistry (a) Design and carry-out a simple research project in Chemistry
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to analyse social and scientific challenges such as water pollutions, water purifications, rusting, fire outbreak, pesticides, acid and chemical waste management Project works: Guide students to apply Chemistry principles to investigate on domestic strategies for water purifications, metals purification, metal extraction. and develop local and scientific mechanisms for addressing the identified scientific challenges
entries.assessment: A simple research project in Chemistry is designed and developed correctly
resources: Library resources and videos, animations or simulations illustrating simple projects in chemistry
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.assessment: A simple research project in Chemistry is designed and developed correctly
resources: Library resources and videos, animations or simulations illustrating simple projects in chemistry
